summary: Uncle Bob argues that stabilization phases—periods where teams let systems run before release to build confidence—are fundamentally a symptom of uncertainty and produce only false confidence. Since production data is entirely new, stabilization behavior has little bearing on production behavior. He contends that teams should attack their uncertainty directly through comprehensive testing strategies including unit tests, acceptance tests, integration tests, and thread-safety measures, progressively reducing stabilization phases until they can be eliminated entirely.
key_points: Stabilization phases exist because development teams have produced code they are not certain of, creating a CYA strategy rather than actual quality assurance
Time running a system is unrelated to quality—it only raises false confidence since production data may drive entirely new code pathways
The logical flaw is believing that execution time indicates quality, when production behavior will differ from stabilization behavior
Teams should proactively attack uncertainty through comprehensive automated testing: unit tests near 100% coverage, acceptance tests, integration tests, and thread-safety strategies
The goal should be progressively decreasing stabilization phases from months to weeks to days to hours, until they can be eliminated entirely
money_quotes: We run them because we are afraid. We are afraid because we are uncertain what the system will do.
There is a certain dissonance between calling ourselves professionals, and being so uncertain about what we have created that we fear what it might do.
The behavior of the system in the stabilization phase has little to no bearing on the behavior of the system in production; because the data coming into the system in production is entirely new data.
So stabilization phases are really just about creating false confidence. They are a CYA strategy.
It will be a long time before I trust software to drive car I am in. Because I – know.
The salesman said: 'Trust the car.' And I thought: 'The hell I will!' And I applied the brakes.
key_insight: Stabilization phases are passive admissions of uncertainty that should be replaced with proactive testing strategies that attack the root causes of that uncertainty.
